findings.title: Pre-fill BAC water volume from prior reconstitution of same compound
findings.current_behavior: User must enter BAC water volume manually each time, even when reconstituting the same vial size of the same compound as before.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When the calculator loads, default the BAC water field to the volume used in the user's most recent reconstitution for that compound (if any). Display a small badge 'Same as last time: 2.0mL' that the user can clear with one tap. Falls back to compound-profile default if no history exists.
findings.user_benefit: Power User reconstituting their 3rd vial of BPC-157 doesn't re-derive the dilution math — they reuse a proven-correct value. Reduces typos in safety-critical field.
findings.cost_reasoning: Single query against existing vial records; one input default plus dismissible badge.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has previously reconstituted this compound, when they open the calculator with this compound selected, then the BAC water field is pre-filled with the most recently used volume and a 'Same as last time' badge is shown that clears the field when tapped.
findings.scope_check: In scope — smart default within existing US-REC-01 flow.

findings.title: Vial expiry default from compound profile shelf-life, not fixed 14 days
findings.current_behavior: US-REC-02 AC1 sets a 14-day default expiry for every reconstituted vial.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Read the 'shelf life reconstituted' field from the compound profile (PRD §5.1) and use that as the default expiry instead of 14 days. Fall back to 14 days only if the profile field is empty. Keep field editable.
findings.user_benefit: Eliminates a class of false 'Expiring soon' warnings for compounds with longer shelf life (e.g., TB-500), and surfaces real risk earlier for compounds with shorter shelf life.
findings.cost_reasoning: One lookup on the compound record; default value substitution.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a compound profile has a 'reconstituted shelf life' value, when a vial of that compound is reconstituted, then the default expiry date is computed from that value rather than the 14-day fallback.
findings.scope_check: In scope — uses existing PRD §5.1 field.

findings.title: Pre-confirm reconstitution summary line before Record
findings.current_behavior: User adjusts inputs then taps 'Record Reconstitution'. No final cross-check display.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Above the Record button, display a single-sentence summary that mirrors how the user would describe it: '5mg vial + 2.0mL BAC = 2.5mg/mL — 10 units gives 250mcg.' This is the read-back step. User taps Record to confirm.
findings.user_benefit: Forces a mental cross-check before committing the vial record. Catches transposed digits ('5mL' instead of '0.5mL') at the moment of highest cost.
findings.cost_reasoning: Reads existing computed values into a single template string; no new logic.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the calculator inputs are valid, when the user is about to record the reconstitution, then a single-sentence summary line is rendered above the Record button restating vial size, BAC volume, resulting concentration, and units-per-target-dose in plain English.
findings.scope_check: In scope — UX refinement of existing US-REC-01/REC-02.

findings.title: Undo toast on individual dose log
findings.current_behavior: Confirming a dose immediately writes the log entry. No undo path beyond manual edit on the same calendar day.
findings.proposed_enhancement: After tap-to-confirm, show a 5-second toast 'Logged 250mcg BPC-157 at 7:14 — Undo'. Tap-undo reverses the write (same calendar day rule still applies). After 5 seconds toast dismisses and audit log is final.
findings.user_benefit: The morning-7am-half-awake mis-tap is the single most common dose-logging error. Saves a manual edit trip into history.
findings.cost_reasoning: Existing edit-within-same-day capability is the backend; just exposes it as an immediate toast action.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has just confirmed a single dose, when the confirmation completes, then a toast with an 'Undo' action appears for 5 seconds. Tapping Undo before dismissal removes the dose log entry (audit event recorded as 'reverted within grace window').
findings.scope_check: In scope — error-recovery UX on existing US-TRK-03.

findings.title: Inline editable dose amount in batch-log review
findings.current_behavior: AC2 allows deselect/skip in batch review but does not allow editing the amount of a single dose without dropping out of the batch flow.
findings.proposed_enhancement: In the batch review list, tap the dose amount to inline-edit (numeric input, defaults to protocol amount). Saves user from cancelling batch, logging individually, then re-batching the remainder.
findings.user_benefit: Common case: 'I took 200mcg instead of 250mcg today because I'm cycling down' — handled in one tap without breaking the 3-minute morning window.
findings.cost_reasoning: Reuses single-dose log's actual-vs-scheduled amount handling.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the batch review list is shown with N pending doses, when the user taps a dose amount, then an inline numeric input opens defaulted to the protocol amount and accepts any positive Decimal value, recorded as 'actual dose' on confirm.
findings.scope_check: In scope — refines existing US-TRK-05 batch flow.

findings.title: Compound profile collapses by persona
findings.current_behavior: Profile shows IUPAC, mechanism, citations, dosing, stacking, side effects — same density for both personas.
findings.proposed_enhancement: For Delegated Participants, render the profile with mechanism/IUPAC/citations collapsed by default and dose + administration route + 'how to inject' visible. Power User sees all expanded. State persisted per user.
findings.user_benefit: Delegated Participant gets the answer they need ('how much, where, how often') without scrolling through pharmacology. Power User keeps current density.
findings.cost_reasoning: Conditional default-expansion on render based on user role; no new content.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a Delegated Participant views a compound profile, when the page renders, then dosing and administration route sections are expanded while mechanism, IUPAC, and citation sections are collapsed behind a 'Show more' control. Power Users see all sections expanded.
findings.scope_check: In scope — progressive disclosure on existing US-REF-01 content.

findings.title: Persistent app-shell sync indicator
findings.current_behavior: AC3 of US-TRK-03 says logs are queued offline and synced on reconnect, but there is no acceptance criterion for surfacing sync state to the user.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Add a small persistent indicator in the app shell header: green dot 'Synced HH:MM', amber dot 'Offline — N queued', red dot 'Sync failed — Retry'. Tappable to view queue contents.
findings.user_benefit: Power User in a basement gym at 7am sees immediately whether their batch log actually made it to the server, or is sitting in a queue they need to confirm.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires a small reactive store hooked to the service worker sync events; one new UI element rendered in shell.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the app shell is rendered, when the user is online and synced, then a green sync indicator with last-sync timestamp is shown. When offline with pending mutations, an amber indicator with queue count is shown. Tapping the indicator opens a list of queued operations.
findings.scope_check: In scope — surfaces existing US-AUT-05 + US-TRK-03 offline behavior.

findings.title: Dashboard empty state on first login
findings.current_behavior: Dashboard renders 'Today's Doses' and inventory badges, but on first login there is no protocol, no inventory, no rating history — risk of an empty grey screen.
findings.proposed_enhancement: First-login dashboard renders a single 'Get started' card with the same 3-step path from the onboarding wizard ('Browse Catalog → Create Protocol → Log First Dose'). Card dismisses once the first protocol exists.
findings.user_benefit: Removes the 'I logged in, now what?' moment for both personas, especially Delegated Participants who arrive via invite link.
findings.cost_reasoning: Single conditional card; ties to existing onboarding wizard content.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has no protocols and no dose logs, when they land on the dashboard, then a 'Get started' card is shown linking to Browse Catalog, Create Protocol, and Log First Dose. The card is hidden once the user has at least one active protocol.
findings.scope_check: In scope — empty-state UX on existing US-ANL-01.

findings.title: Numeric input mode + decimal hint on all dose fields
findings.current_behavior: Stories specify dose amount inputs but don't constrain input UX for mobile.
findings.proposed_enhancement: All dose-amount and BAC-volume inputs use inputmode='decimal' with explicit Decimal validation. iOS shows the decimal keyboard rather than the integer spinner. Add aria-describedby pointing to a unit hint ('mcg', 'mL').
findings.user_benefit: Power User entering '0.5mL' on a phone gets the right keyboard immediately; reduces transposition errors caused by toggling between number and symbol keyboards.
findings.cost_reasoning: Input attributes only.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a dose-amount or BAC-volume input is rendered, when the user focuses it on a mobile device, then the decimal numeric keypad is shown and an accessible label describing the unit is associated with the field.
findings.scope_check: In scope — accessibility on existing stories.

findings.title: Warning badges combine color + icon + text
findings.current_behavior: Multiple stories rely on yellow/red badges (low inventory, expiring, high-volume warning).
findings.proposed_enhancement: Every warning badge must combine color, icon (e.g., triangle for warning), and text label. No warning is conveyed by color alone. Meets WCAG 1.4.1.
findings.user_benefit: Power Users with red/green or yellow/orange color vision deficiency see the warning. Also helps when the screen is bright outdoors.
findings.cost_reasoning: Design-system rule applied to existing badge component.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given any warning or status badge is rendered (low inventory, expiring vial, dose-above-range, etc.), when it is displayed, then the badge includes a color, an icon, and a text label — no warning is conveyed by color alone.
findings.scope_check: In scope — a11y polish on existing badges.

findings.title: Show days-since-last-use at each rotation site
findings.current_behavior: AC2 shows the last 7 sites used; AC1 suggests next site round-robin.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When showing the rotation suggestion, render each candidate site with 'last used N days ago' as a small subtitle. Sites unused for ≥7 days get a subtle 'rested' indicator. User override decisions become better informed.
findings.user_benefit: Power User overriding the suggestion (because they have a bruise on the recommended site) can pick the most-rested alternative without scanning history.
findings.cost_reasoning: Existing dose-log query already retrieved; render extra metadata.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a dose is being logged with site rotation available, when the site picker is shown, then each candidate site displays 'last used N days ago' (or 'never' if unused), and sites unused ≥7 days are marked as rested.
findings.scope_check: In scope — enhances existing US-TRK-04.

findings.title: Wallet address character-diff highlight on payment gate
findings.current_behavior: AC4 shows the previous wallet address for comparison and requires the user to verify the current address from the vendor's reply.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When AC4's stale-wallet comparison is displayed, render the previous and current addresses with character-by-character diff highlighting (red strike-through on removed chars, green on added). Identical chars are de-emphasized. Makes 'this address looks the same as last time' visually obvious vs 'one character is different — verify intentional'.
findings.user_benefit: Direct defense against the most expensive failure mode: paying to a similar-looking but different wallet. The diff makes character substitution attacks visible in 1 second.
findings.cost_reasoning: Need a small diff utility + dedicated component; testing the diff logic adds work.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a previous wallet address exists for this vendor, when the payment confirmation gate renders, then the new address and the previous address are displayed in a side-by-side character diff view highlighting differences. The 'Mark Payment Sent' button stays disabled until the user has tapped 'I have compared the addresses'.
findings.scope_check: In scope — refinement of existing US-ORD-04 safety gate.

findings.title: Wallet address must be retyped, not pasted, on first use per vendor
findings.current_behavior: User enters wallet address from vendor's reply. No restriction on paste.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On the first transaction to a new vendor wallet, the address field disables paste — the user must type the address. On subsequent orders to the same vendor + same address, paste is permitted. Reduces risk of clipboard-poisoning malware substituting a wallet address.
findings.user_benefit: Defends against a known crypto-theft vector (clipboard hijacking) on first contact, while not annoying the user on repeat orders.
findings.cost_reasoning: Custom input behavior; per-vendor known-address state; user override path.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the user is entering a wallet address that has not previously been confirmed for this vendor, when they attempt to paste, then the paste action is blocked and a hint reads 'Type this wallet address by hand the first time — paste available on repeat orders.'
findings.scope_check: In scope — safety refinement on US-ORD-04. Backlogged because of UX-friction tradeoff.

findings.title: Recently-viewed compounds float to top of catalog
findings.current_behavior: AC1/AC2 describe text-search and category filter on the catalog.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Above the search/filter, render a 'Recently viewed' row with the user's last 5 distinct compound profiles. No configuration needed. Quietly disappears for new users with no history.
findings.user_benefit: Power User cycling through 5-7 protocols can re-reach the compound they were looking up yesterday without typing.
findings.cost_reasoning: Read existing CompoundProfileViewed events; render a row.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has viewed at least one compound profile, when they enter the catalog screen, then a 'Recently viewed' row showing their last 5 distinct compounds is rendered above the search and filter controls.
findings.scope_check: In scope — leverages existing CompoundProfileViewed event.

findings.title: Clipboard-assisted vendor reply capture (regex, no AI)
findings.current_behavior: AC3 of US-ORD-09 lets user proceed to payment gate and manually enter total, currency, wallet address.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On the payment gate, offer a 'Paste vendor reply' multi-line text box. When the user pastes Telegram message text, the app applies non-AI regex heuristics to highlight detected wallet-address-like strings, currency tokens (USDT/BTC/ETH/USD), and amount patterns ($123.45). User taps each highlighted token to copy it into the structured fields. No AI; deterministic regex only.
findings.user_benefit: Saves the Power User from manually copying three values from a long Telegram message. v2 AI parser becomes a smaller delta on top.
findings.cost_reasoning: Regex patterns + token-tap-to-fill UI; manageable test surface.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the user is on the payment confirmation gate, when they paste Telegram message text into a 'Paste vendor reply' field, then detected wallet-address, currency, and amount tokens are highlighted in the pasted text and each highlighted token can be tapped to populate the corresponding structured field below.
findings.scope_check: In scope — non-AI heuristic; doesn't add v2 AI parser. Backlogged because v2 parser supersedes it.

findings.title: Skeleton states + lazy-load citations on compound profile
findings.current_behavior: Profile loads with mechanism, dosing, citations, side-effects all in one render.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Render dosing + administration route + sourcing note in the first paint (within 200ms target). Lazy-load citations and side-effect details below the fold. Use skeleton placeholders during fetch.
findings.user_benefit: On a slow mobile network mid-7am routine, the user gets the dose and site information instantly, while the deeper profile content loads progressively.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires splitting profile fetch into above-fold / below-fold queries.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a compound profile is requested, when the page renders, then dosing range, administration route, and current vial/protocol status are visible within 200ms of first paint, and citations and side effects load progressively below the fold with skeleton placeholders.
findings.scope_check: In scope — performance refinement on US-REF-01.

findings.title: Order draft survives Telegram send failure
findings.current_behavior: PRD §5.4.3 says 'If MTProto send fails, show error, automatically surface fallback mode, do NOT silently discard the order.'
findings.proposed_enhancement: Make the persistence explicit in the user story: a 'Send queue' for orders whose Telegram send failed. Each queued order shows error reason + retry button + fallback (copy message / open deep-link). Power User can reopen the app hours later and find the failed order intact.
findings.user_benefit: If the user's Telegram session expires mid-flow at 11pm, the work they did building the cart isn't lost. Reopening the app the next morning surfaces the queued order with retry path.
findings.cost_reasoning: Adds a persistent queue entity + UI surface.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a Telegram send attempt fails, when the user navigates away and returns later, then the order remains in 'Send failed' state with the composed message intact, a retry action, and the manual fallback options (copy message, open Telegram deep-link) still available.
findings.scope_check: In scope — extends US-ORD-03 error path to be persistent.

findings.title: Outcome-log tag presets from recent usage
findings.current_behavior: AC1 lets the user pick tags from a list (Energy, Pain, etc).
findings.proposed_enhancement: Above the full tag list, show a row of the user's 3 most-used tags from the last 14 days as one-tap presets. The full list remains accessible. No AI; pure recency frequency.
findings.user_benefit: Power User logging 'energy, sleep' daily doesn't re-scroll the tag list each time.
findings.cost_reasoning: Frequency query on OutcomeLogged events; row render.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has logged outcome entries with tags in the past 14 days, when they open the outcome-logging UI, then their 3 most-frequent tags from the period are shown as one-tap presets above the full tag selector.
findings.scope_check: In scope — leverages existing OutcomeLogged events.

findings.title: Copy-invite-link action alongside email send
findings.current_behavior: AC1 sends an email invite link.
findings.proposed_enhancement: After the invite is created, show the link inline with a copy-to-clipboard button. Power User can also share via Signal, Telegram, SMS, AirDrop — wherever family member actually responds. Email is sent in parallel; both paths land in the same one-time link.
findings.user_benefit: Family members often miss app invite emails (spam folder, wrong address). Copy-link is a reliable side-channel.
findings.cost_reasoning: Show the existing link in UI; clipboard write.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given an invite has been generated for a managed user, when the admin views the invite-status row, then the invite link is shown alongside a copy-to-clipboard action; the email is still sent in parallel.
findings.scope_check: In scope — augments existing US-ADM-01 path.

findings.title: Stale-data badge on inventory and adherence when offline
findings.current_behavior: When offline, the dashboard renders the last cached data without distinguishing it from live data.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When offline OR when cache is older than 30 minutes, display a subtle 'Last refreshed HH:MM' badge on inventory, adherence, and dashboard numeric tiles. Optionally cross-hatch the tile background. Removes ambiguity about whether 'low inventory' is current.
findings.user_benefit: Power User building an order in airplane mode knows the inventory numbers might be stale and waits to confirm at home.
findings.cost_reasoning: Cache timestamp + conditional badge render.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the user is offline or the displayed data was fetched more than 30 minutes ago, when inventory, adherence, or dashboard tiles are rendered, then each tile shows a 'Last refreshed HH:MM' indicator.
findings.scope_check: In scope — UX of existing offline behavior.

findings.title: Batch-log success haptic + checkmark animation
findings.current_behavior: AC1 logs all scheduled doses; no specified completion feedback.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On successful batch confirm, fire a single short haptic pulse on mobile + a checkmark sweep animation across each logged dose row. Collapses to 'Today: 3/3 complete ✓' summary card.
findings.user_benefit: The morning-routine close ritual. Confirms the most important action of the day with sensory feedback that the dose-and-go cycle is finished.
findings.cost_reasoning: Web Vibration API call + CSS animation.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a batch dose confirmation succeeds, when the operation completes, then a short haptic pulse fires (where supported) and each logged dose row plays a checkmark animation before collapsing into a 'Today: N/N complete' summary card.
findings.scope_check: In scope — micro-interaction on existing US-TRK-05.

findings.title: Visible 'next reminder' time on dashboard
findings.current_behavior: AC1 sets a reminder time; reminders fire as push/email but the configured time isn't surfaced on the dashboard.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On the dashboard footer or in the doses card, display 'Next reminder: 7:00 AM' so the user can verify their setting at a glance. Tap to edit the time directly.
findings.user_benefit: Removes the 'did I save my reminder?' uncertainty. Especially useful on first setup and after timezone changes.
findings.cost_reasoning: Reads existing reminder setting; passive render.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has a daily reminder time configured, when they view the dashboard, then 'Next reminder: HH:MM' is displayed and tapping it opens the reminder time editor inline.
findings.scope_check: In scope — surfaces existing US-TRK-09 configuration.

findings.title: Catalog search learns user's synonyms
findings.current_behavior: AC1 does substring text search on compound name.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Maintain a per-user search-alias table built passively from past searches: if the user typed 'glp' then selected 'Semaglutide', remember the alias. Next time 'glp' surfaces Semaglutide as a top result without any user configuration. No AI model required; deterministic ranking.
findings.user_benefit: Power User builds their own shortcuts naturally. Delegated Participant unaffected.
findings.cost_reasoning: Persistent alias table + rank boost in search query.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a user has previously selected compound X after typing query Q, when they type Q again, then compound X is ranked above other substring matches.
findings.scope_check: In scope — search refinement on US-REF-02. Backlog because not required for v1 launch.

findings.title: Same-as-current rejection visible at typing time
findings.current_behavior: AC3 rejects identical new-password at submit time.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Validate same-as-current at blur time on the new-password field, surfacing 'Must differ from current password' before the user fills out the confirm-password field. Don't reveal hash details — just structurally check.
findings.user_benefit: Avoids a wasted-typing trip through 12+ characters in two fields before learning the password is rejected.
findings.cost_reasoning: One round-trip same-as-current check on blur, gated behind current-password verification done in the same submit.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the user has entered their current password and is typing a new password, when they blur the new-password field with a value identical to the current password, then a 'Must differ from current password' message is shown before they reach the confirm-password field.
findings.scope_check: In scope — refinement of US-AUT-06.
